records.ety: [L. interfusus, p. p. of interfundere to pour between; inter between + fundere to pour. See Fuse to melt.]

defs: To pour or spread between or among; to diffuse; to scatter.
quotes: The ambient air, wide interfused, Embracing round this florid earth.

defs: To spread through; to permeate; to pervade.
quotes: Keats, in whom the moral seems to have so perfectly interfused the physical man, that you might almost say he could feel sorrow with his hands.

defs: To mix up together; to associate.
